mechanism_narrative: Steady expansion in nonfarm payrolls signals sustained economic growth, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines for two consecutive months and SPX still posts positive returns, or if rolling 6-month correlation between PAYEMS change and SPX forward returns is less than 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above a threshold signals recessionary turn, hurting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises >0.5pp in 3 months but SPX still posts positive returns in 60% of such instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below inflation rate (negative real rate) and falling, it signals accommodative policy that boosts equities over the next 20 days, requiring joint confirmation from both series.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL year-over-year change (real rate negative)
regime_hint: monetary easing or negative real rate regime
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening construction and broader slowdown, leading to negative equity returns, particularly during late-cycle expansion when GDP is above trend.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion when GDPC1 is above its 5-year trend
failure_condition: If HOUST YoY decline >10% but SPX 60-day return is positive in >50% of cases during late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production signals strong manufacturing and capex, which supports corporate earnings and stock prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO monthly growth is positive but SPX 20-day return is negative in >55% of months, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand, leading to immediate negative repricing in equities over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS MoM decline >1% but SPX 5-day return is positive in >50% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls and retail sales indicates a virtuous employment-consumption cycle that strongly boosts equity markets over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and RSAFS have positive month-over-month growth for the latest month
failure_condition: If both series rise MoM but SPX 60-day return is negative in >40% of such occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising over the past 6 months), further rate hikes suppress equity valuations via higher discount rates, leading to negative returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS level is higher than 6 months prior (i.e., tightening cycle)
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is in tightening mode but SPX 20-day return is positive in >55% of instances, reject.
